title: Fixing Node.js Express Middleware for Route Parameter Validation in Nested Routes

statement.markdown: You are given a Node.js Express application with a middleware function to validate route parameters in nested routes. However, the current implementation has a logical bug that causes incorrect validation for certain routes. Your task is to fix the bug in the middleware function to ensure that it correctly validates route parameters in nested routes.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a JSON object representing the route parameters.
statement.outputFormat: The output will be a boolean indicating whether the route parameters are valid.
statement.notes: The middleware function should recursively validate the route parameters in nested routes. You can assume that the route parameters are represented as a nested JSON object.

examples.explanation: The route parameters are not valid because the zip code is missing

hints.text: First hint text: Start by analyzing the current middleware function to understand the logical bug.
